# 7.3 Visualizations

This section presents key visualizations generated throughout the analysis process to effectively communicate model performance, data insights, and trends. The goal is to provide a clear and compelling visual representation of our findings.

*   **1. Model Performance Comparison:** [Describe the type of chart(s) used – e.g., “A scatter plot comparing predicted vs. actual values was generated for each model.” or "Box plots were created to visualize the distribution of residuals."] Reference Figure [X].
*   **2. Feature Importance Visualization:** [Detail any visualizations showing feature importance - e.g., “A bar chart illustrating the relative importance of features in the Gradient Boosting Model was included.”] Reference Figure [Y].
*   **3. Data Distribution Visualizations:** [Describe any charts used to illustrate data distributions – e.g., "Histograms were generated for key numerical features to assess skewness and identify potential outliers." or “A stacked bar chart displayed the distribution of categorical variables.”] Reference Figure [Z].
*   **4. (Optional) Other Relevant Visualizations:** [Include any other visualizations that support your analysis – e.g., "Correlation matrices were used to visualize relationships between features."] Reference Figure [A].
